summary: PR 2616 splits Kirby attack-dash code out of broader Kirby translation units into a new matching object `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c`, adds its header, updates split ranges and build configuration, and redirects includes from the broad `ftkirby.h` header to the narrower attack-dash header where needed.

key_files.role: New split source file containing Kirby attack-dash and attack-dash-air functions from 0x800F1F68 through 0x800F21C0, with two local static callback helpers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new file `ftkirbyattackdash.c` with 102 added lines

key_files.role: New narrow public header for externally referenced Kirby attack-dash functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new file `ftkirbyattackdash.h` with 17 added lines

key_files.role: Split metadata updated to carve `ftkirbyattackdash.c` out between `ftkirby.c` and `ftkirbyspecialn.c`, including text and sdata2 ranges.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `ftkirby.c` text end changed from 0x800F1FDC to 0x800F1F68
diff_excerpt: `ftkirbyspecialn.c` text start changed from 0x800F1FDC to 0x800F21E8 and sdata2 start from 0x804D9364 to 0x804D9370

key_files.role: Build configuration adds the new Kirby attack-dash object as `Matching`.
Original host file has attack-dash functions removed, include surface adjusted, and two switch defaults added.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: attack-dash functions removed from this file
diff_excerpt: `ftCommon/ftCo_AttackDash.h` include removed and `ftKirby/inlines.h` include added
diff_excerpt: default break cases added in `ftKb_CaSpecialN_Phys` and `ftKb_CaSpecialAirN_Phys`

key_files.role: Original Kirby source no longer owns `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68`; it includes the new attack-dash header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68` and extern float declarations removed

key_files.role: Broad Kirby header stops declaring attack-dash functions now owned by the new narrow header.
Consumer include updated from broad Kirby header to the new attack-dash header.
Item-side consumer include updated to use the new attack-dash header; unrelated item include surface adjusted.

what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c` as a new source file for Kirby attack-dash behavior.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.h` and moved public attack-dash prototypes out of `ftkirby.h`.
Marked the new `ftkirbyattackdash.c` object as `Matching` in `configure.py`.
Adjusted GALE01 split boundaries so `ftkirbyattackdash.c` occupies `.text 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8` and `.sdata2 0x804D9368-0x804D9370`.
Removed attack-dash functions from `ftkirbyspecialn.c` and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68` from `ftkirby.c` as part of the split.
Replaced broad Kirby header includes with `ftkirbyattackdash.h` in consumers that need attack-dash declarations.
In the new split file, `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4` are local static callbacks while exported attack-dash functions remain in the new header.
Changed `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68` from using external `ftKb_Init_804D9368`/`ftKb_Init_804D936C` constants to inline float literals `0.0f` and `1.0f` in the new file.

smart_moves.text: Created a narrow header for the newly split attack-dash surface instead of keeping these declarations in the broad Kirby header.
Kept callback-only helpers local to the new translation unit by declaring `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4` static in `ftkirbyattackdash.c` and omitting them from the public header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `ftkirbyattackdash.h` only declares public attack-dash functions

smart_moves.text: Updated split metadata and build configuration together with the source split, keeping text/sdata2 ranges and `Matching` object registration aligned.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a contiguous function range is split into its own matching object, update both split metadata and build object classification in the same PR slice, and move only the declarations needed by external consumers into a narrow owning header.
For split cleanup, remove declarations from broad headers once the split-specific header owns them, then update direct consumers to include the narrower header.
If helper callbacks are only used inside the newly split file, keep them file-local rather than exporting them through the split header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `static void fn_800F1FDC` and `static void fn_800F20C4` in `ftkirbyattackdash.c`
diff_excerpt: those two helpers are not listed in `ftkirbyattackdash.h`

naming_conventions.text: The new file and header use the lowercase path/name pattern `ftkirbyattackdash.c` and `ftkirbyattackdash.h`, while public functions retain the existing `ftKb_AttackDash*` and `ftKb_AttackDashAir*` names with address comments.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: prototypes retain names such as `ftKb_AttackDash_Anim` and `ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F21C0`

naming_conventions.text: Unidentified local callback helpers retain address-style names (`fn_800F1FDC`, `fn_800F20C4`) instead of being given speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: static helper declarations and definitions in `ftkirbyattackdash.c`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Split metadata isolates a contiguous text range from 0x800F1F68 to 0x800F21E8 and the corresponding sdata2 range from 0x804D9368 to 0x804D9370 for the new object.
The new object is explicitly registered as `Matching` in `configure.py`; the PR evidence does not include local build, objdiff, or regression output beyond that classification.
evidence_refs: metadata_json: no review comments or issue comments; no validation logs included

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Float constants formerly referenced via extern sdata2 symbols in `ftkirby.c` are represented as inline literals in the new split source.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: removed `extern const f32 ftKb_Init_804D9368` and `ftKb_Init_804D936C`

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c` for cleanup or validation after the split.
Search for `ftkirbyattackdash.h` include usage to confirm consumers stayed narrow after PR 2616.
Search later changes around split ranges `0x800F1F68` and `0x800F21E8` for any corrections to `splits.txt`.
Search for `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4` to see whether callback names were later recovered or kept address-style.
Search for later PRs replacing extern sdata2 float constants with inline literals in split fighter files.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR title, author, merge timestamp, changed file list, counts
raw_diff: complete diff available and not truncated
notes: Evidence is strong for files changed, split ranges, object classification, include updates, and moved functions because the complete diff is loaded.
Evidence is weak for author intent beyond the title and diff because the PR body is empty and there are no issue or review comments.
No build logs, objdiff output, or regression results are included; the record notes `configure.py` marks the object as `Matching` but does not independently claim validation.
No review feedback was available in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow header ownership during fighter TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2616 supports a reusable pattern for fighter translation-unit splits: move the split-specific public prototypes into a new narrow header and update external consumers to include that header, while removing those prototypes from the broad character header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `ftCo_AttackDash.c` and `itkirbycutterbeam.c` include the new header

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep split-local callbacks static
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2616 supports keeping callback helpers local to the new split source when they are only used inside that file, rather than exporting address-style helper declarations through a public header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4` are declared static in `ftkirbyattackdash.c`
diff_excerpt: these helpers are omitted from `ftkirbyattackdash.h`

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby attack-dash split object path and ranges from PR 2616
source_update_candidates.text: `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c` was added as a matching object with `.text` range 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8 and `.sdata2` range 0x804D9368-0x804D9370 in GALE01 splits.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-owned path/split fact suitable for curator review, not an intake-promoted global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Attack-dash declarations moved from `ftkirby.h` to `ftkirbyattackdash.h`
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2616 moved public Kirby attack-dash prototypes into `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.h` and removed them from `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirby.h`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Header ownership is a path/source fact that may help future include cleanup and symbol lookup.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting narrow-header cleanup for split objects
source_update_candidates.text: When carving a focused matching object out of a broader fighter TU, consider moving only that object's externally needed declarations into a narrow owning header and updating direct consumers away from broad character headers.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: consumers changed from broad `ftkirby.h` to attack-dash header
source_update_candidates.reason: The pattern is supported by this PR, but curator should decide whether it belongs in global standards or only as a local lesson.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or requested changes; there are no review comments in the slice.
Do not claim objdiff/build/regression validation from this intake; the slice only shows `configure.py` classifying the new object as `Matching`.
Do not generalize that all sdata2 float constants should be inlined; this PR only shows that replacement for `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68` in the new split source.
Do not rename or semantically interpret `fn_800F1FDC` and `fn_800F20C4`; the PR keeps address-style names.
